sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Follow-up for chronic congestive heart failure with diastolic dysfunction and planned knee surgery.
History of Present Illness
The patient presents for follow-up regarding chronic congestive heart failure, initially managed with furosemide and torsemide (20 mg daily). He reports increasing dyspnea and has been tolerating the current regimen well. He occasionally experiences leg cramps, which he attributes to cold exposure. He discontinued a previous proton pump inhibitor and reports tolerating the current medication. The patient is scheduled for a right knee replacement at the end of June and desires a colonoscopy prior to surgery. He denies a family history of colon cancer. He is currently taking lisinopril at 10 mg daily.
Findings
The patient's BMP has been ranging from 3,000 to 6,000 in March and April, with a potassium of 3.9 and a creatinine of 0.7. His BUN is slightly elevated at 23. His last a1c was 5.5 in March, and today's A1c is 5.1. Magnesium levels are normal at 1.7. Blood pressure is well controlled. An EKG is being repeated today. There is no evidence of recent chest imaging. Lab results are otherwise unremarkable.
Assessment
The patient presents with chronic congestive heart failure, managed with torsemide and lisinopril. He is awaiting knee surgery and requires pre-operative evaluation, including a colonoscopy.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: The patient will continue his current regimen of torsemide and lisinopril. A pre-operative EKG will be obtained. A colonoscopy will be arranged at Bartley Regional Hospital or Valley Medical in Springfield, prioritizing completion within one month of surgery. Follow-up will be scheduled in four weeks if symptoms persist. The patient will be instructed to discontinue clopidogrel one week prior to surgery.
